valid.name: concurrent
valid.code: CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_users_email ON users (email);

valid.name: never-without-concurrently
valid.code:
CREATE INDEX idx_users_email ON users (email);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_users_uid ON users (uid);

errors.message: Use `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY`. A plain `CREATE INDEX` takes a `SHARE` lock on the target table for the duration of the build — readers are unaffected, but every writer is blocked. Concurrent index builds cannot run inside a transaction, so a migration tool that wraps each step in BEGIN/COMMIT needs an explicit opt-out here.

invalid.name: never-with-concurrently
invalid.code:
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_users_email ON users (email);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_users_uid ON users (uid);

errors.message: Avoid `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY`. Concurrent index builds cannot run inside a transaction, so they conflict with migration tools that wrap each step in BEGIN/COMMIT; drop the keyword and use a plain `CREATE INDEX`.
